title: Environmental Nucleic Acid Extraction from Filter Samples Using MagMAX Kit
author: Yoko Makabe-Kobayashi
materials: MagMAX Microbiome Ultra Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit

steps.instruction: Push out the RNAlater from the Sterivex cartridge with 10 mL syringe (TERUMO, SS-10SZ).

Open the Sterivex cartridg by a tube cutter. Using a surgical scalpel (Akiyama MEDICAL MFG, Replacement blade No. 15) and two sets of sterile forceps, pick up the filter membrane at opposite edges and transfer the filter into a 5 mL tube (TOMY, TM-657S) with the top side inward.
Add the homogenization beads (provided in the kit) into the 5 mL tube.
Add 800 µL of the Lysis Buffer to the 5 mL tubes. 800 µL

Place the 5 mL tubes into Microsmash Beads Beater (TOMY, MS-100). Shake at speed 3,800 rpm for 1 minute and then rest for 30 seconds, then 3,800 rpm for 1 minute. 3800 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute 00:00:30 3800 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute Microsmash Beads Beater (TOMY, MS-100, https://bio.tomys.co.jp/products/micro_homogenizing_system/ )
Centrifuge the 5 mL tubes at 5000×g for 5 minutes at room temperature. 5000 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes

Transfer the supernatant to a clean 2 mL Tube (VIOLAMO MicroTube 1-1600-04). It will be necessary to push the tip of pipett through the beads into the bottom of the tube in order to recover as much supernatant as possible. Note: However, don't transfer the beads as much as possible. <STOPPING POINT> Bead tube can be stored at 4°C overnight after lysis.

Invert the Binding Bead Mix to mix, then add 520 µL to each tubes. 520 µL Note: Remix the Binding Bead Mix by inversion frequently during pipetting to ensure even distribution of beads to all samples or wells. The mixture containing the Binding Beads is viscous. Therefore, pipet slowly to ensure that the correct amount is added. DO NOT use a repeat pipet to add to the samples as the high viscosity will cause variations in volume added. Note: Centrifuge the tube at 800 rpm for 1 minute to collect liquid to bottom of tube if needed.

Shake the tube at 900 rpm for 5 minutes. 900 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes Digital VORTEX-GENIE 2 (Scientific Industries, Inc, SI-A236, https://www.scientificindustries.com/digital-vortex-genie-2.html ) Tube Support for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 504-0234-00) 6 Platform Head, for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 146-6005-00)

Place the tube on the magnetic stand for at least 5 minutes, or until all the beads have collected 00:05:00 Room temperature DynaMag™-2 Magnet (Magnetic tube rack, Invitrogen™, 12321D, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/12321D#:~:text=The%20DynaMag%E2%84%A2%2D2%20magnet%20combines%20a%20strong%20magnetic%20attraction,microcentrifuge%20tubes%20in%20numbered%20spaces )

Keeping the tube on the magnet, carefully open the lid, then discard the supernatant. Note: Be sure to completely remove any contaminating homogenization beads. IMPORTANT! Avoid disturbing the magnet beads.
Remove the tube from the magnetic stand, then add 1 mL of the Wash Buffer to each sample. 1 mL

Shake at 800 rpm for 30 seconds. 800 rpm, Room temperature, 30 seconds Digital VORTEX-GENIE 2 (Scientific Industries, Inc, SI-A236, https://www.scientificindustries.com/digital-vortex-genie-2.html ) Tube Support for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 504-0234-00) 6 Platform Head, for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 146-6005-00)

Place the tube on the magnetic stand for 3 minutes, or until all the beads have collected. 00:03:00 DynaMag™-2 Magnet (Magnetic tube rack, Invitrogen™, 12321D, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/12321D#:~:text=The%20DynaMag%E2%84%A2%2D2%20magnet%20combines%20a%20strong%20magnetic%20attraction,microcentrifuge%20tubes%20in%20numbered%20spaces )
Keeping the tube on the magnet, carefully open the lid, then discard the supernatant from each well. IMPORTANT! Avoid disturbing the magnet beads.
Repeat from step 12 to step 15 using 1 mL of 80% Ethanol . 1 mL 00:03:30
Dry the beads with open the cap for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00 Room temperature
Add 50 µL of the Elution Solution to each tubes. 50 µL
Place the tube on heat-block at 75°C for 5 minutes. 75 °C 00:05:00

Place the tube on a shaker at 800 rpm for 5 minutes. 800 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes Digital VORTEX-GENIE 2 (Scientific Industries, Inc, SI-A236, https://www.scientificindustries.com/digital-vortex-genie-2.html ) Tube Support for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 504-0234-00) 6 Platform Head, for Vortex GENIE2 (Scientific Industries, Inc., 146-6005-00)

Place the tube on the magnetic stand for 3 minutes or until all beads are collected against the magnets. 00:03:00 DynaMag™-2 Magnet (Magnetic tube rack, Invitrogen™, 12321D, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/12321D#:~:text=The%20DynaMag%E2%84%A2%2D2%20magnet%20combines%20a%20strong%20magnetic%20attraction,microcentrifuge%20tubes%20in%20numbered%20spaces )
Keep the tube on the magnet and carefully open the lid, then transfer the eluates to a new 1.5 mL tube.
The purified nucleic acid is ready for immediate use. Alternatively, store at −20°C for up to 6 months and −80°C for greater than 6 months.
For DNA sample: Phenol-chloroform extraction (PCI purification) is performed after RNase treatment. For RNA sample: TURBO DNA- free ‱ Kit (Invitrogen, AM1907) is performed.
Repeat from step 12 to step 15 using 1 mL of the Wash Buffer . 1 mL 00:03:30
Repeat from step 12 to step 15 using 1 mL of 80% Ethanol . 1 mL 00:03:30
